name: SACROCOCCYGEAL TERATOMA
dominant_finding: amorphous, punctate, spiculated calcifications, possibly resembling bone (36-50%);
discriminating_features: amorphous, punctate, spiculated calcifications, possibly resembling bone (36-50%);
soft-tissue mass in pelvis protruding anteriorly + inferiorly

general: amorphous, punctate, spiculated calcifications, possibly resembling bone (36-50%);
soft-tissue mass in pelvis protruding anteriorly + inferiorly
ANGIO: neovascularity (arterial supply by middle + lateral sacral + gluteal branches of internal
early venous filling with serpiginous dilated tumor veins
poor prognostic factors : oligohydramnios, fetal hydrops with ascites, fetal hydronephrosis,
increased size of uterus ← mass / polyhydramnios
assessment of tumor volume, amniotic fluid index, placental thickness, diameter of IVC,
lobulated + sharply demarcated tumor extremely heterogeneous on T1WI ← high signal
best modality to detect spinal canal invasion

clinical.Rx: US-guided drainage of cystic component, fetal surgery + Cesarean section for solid
clinical.Prognosis: likely benign: predominantly fatty / cystic tumor
